title: Adopt a Retail Mac into ABM (Apple Configurator)

steps.title: Check the Mac qualifies
steps.instructions: Apple silicon or a T2 chip, macOS 12.0.1 or later. Older Intel Macs without T2 cannot be adopted - keep them on /mdm Jamf manual.

steps.title: Prepare the iPhone
steps.instructions: Install Apple Configurator from the App Store. Sign in with the Managed Apple ID.

steps.title: Preset auto-assignment in ABM (one time)
steps.instructions: ABM > Settings > Device Management Settings > Devices added by Apple Configurator > assign to the Jamf server. With this set, adopted Macs skip manual assignment.

steps.title: Erase the Mac to Setup Assistant
steps.instructions: New in box: just power on. Used: System Settings > General > Transfer or Reset > Erase All Content and Settings. Join Wi-Fi during setup.
steps.done_when: Mac sits at the country/region pane.

steps.title: Pair with the iPhone
steps.instructions: Hold the iPhone near the Mac at the country/region pane. The Mac shows a pairing animation; scan it with the iPhone camera in Configurator.
steps.done_when: Device visible in ABM > Devices, assigned to Jamf.

steps.title: Restart Setup Assistant and enroll
steps.instructions: Erase again if setup went past the region pane. This time the Remote Management screen appears and the Mac enrolls into Jamf. From now on this Mac uses /mdm Jamf auto in the Workstation SOP.
steps.done_when: Mac shows in Jamf as managed.

meta.why: Retail-bought Macs have no Apple Business Manager record, so zero-touch enrollment (ADE) can never happen. Adoption gives the Mac an ABM record - after this it enrolls like a business-bought machine, on every future rebuild.
meta.how: Pair the erased Mac with an iPhone running Apple Configurator at Setup Assistant; ABM records the device; Jamf takes it from there.
meta.tools:
iPhone (iOS 15+) with the Apple Configurator app
iPhone signed in with a Managed Apple ID - Administrator or Device Enrollment Manager role
Wi-Fi both devices can reach
meta.safety:
The Mac must be erased - adoption only works from Setup Assistant. Back up anything on it first.
30-day provisional period: the device can be released from ABM during it. Keep the Mac in IT hands until enrollment is confirmed.
